short_summary: New PM nominated, regional tensions persist
rationale: Romania's Liberal former mayor nominated as PM is the dominant domestic story; EU-Ukraine accession talks and Beirut/Iran tensions remain background continuations with indirect regional relevance.

top_events.title: Romanian president picks Liberal former mayor as PM to form new government
top_events.summary: Romania's prolonged government formation process reaches a new step with a Liberal PM nominee, directly affecting domestic policy direction and EU fund access for Romanian residents.

top_events.title: Trump says US-Iran deal to be signed Sunday; Israel strikes Beirut
top_events.summary: Continued uncertainty over the Iran deal and Israeli strikes on Beirut sustain energy price volatility affecting Romanian households indirectly through EU gas markets.

top_events.title: EU set to hold membership talks with Ukraine
top_events.summary: Advancing EU-Ukraine accession talks are a continuation of a known process; modest positive signal for Romania's eastern neighborhood stability but no new qualitative leap.
